request.description:
Use this endpoint to fetch valid **location** values used in the `params` of the **Google Search Scraper**. These values provide geographic bias for your search queries.

---

### 🧾 Query Parameters

| Parameter        | Required | Description                                                         |
| ---------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **q**            | ✅ Yes   | Substring to search for in region, district, or city names.         |
| **country_name** | ✅ Yes   | Full country name to scope the search (e.g., `United States`).      |

---

### 📄 Response Fields

| Field             | Type   | Description                                                                                 |
| ----------------- | ------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **name**          | string | Display name of the location (region, district, or city).                                   |
| **canonical_name**| string | Full hierarchical name including its parent contexts (e.g., `"Alabama,United States"`).     |
| **country_name**  | string | Country associated with this location (matches your `country_name` query).                  |
| **country_code**  | string | ISO 2-letter country code for the location’s country (e.g., `US`).                          |

---

### 📎 Use In Context

- Use each returned **name** as the value for your `params.location` when running the Google Search Scraper.
- Ensures you supply a valid, recognized geographic bias (e.g., state, district, or city).
- Combine with other `params` (like `country`, `language`, `max_pages`, etc.) to build precise and accurate search tasks.

response.name: Example

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Google Search Scraper (2)",
  "concurrency": 1,
  "export_unique_results": true,
  "to_complete": false,
  "no_line_breaks": true,
  "params": {
    "country": "United States",
    "language": "English",
    "max_pages": 4,
    "mobile_results": true,
    "results_per_page": 10,
    "location": "Alabama"
  }
}

request.description: ➡️ These parameters are specific to the **Google Search Scraper**. _Refer to the_ [Constructing the params Object](#13eb068f-2bae-4603-941e-9f36038fb379) _section to understand how to format and apply them._ | Parameter | Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | **max_pages** | integer | Maximum number of Google Search result pages crawled per URL. Google limits total results to \~300–400. | | **mobile_results** | boolean | If `true`, returns results as if from a mobile device; otherwise returns desktop results by default. | | **results_per_page** | integer | Number of search results per page. Allowed values: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 100. Defaults to 10. | | **country** | string | Sets the IP‐origin country and Google domain (e.g., `google.fr` for France). Default: `United States`. | | **location** | string | Geographic bias for search results (e.g., a state or city). | | **language** | string | Language for search results (overrides the country default). Defaults to English for the chosen country. | --- ### ⚠️ **Additional Notes** - To discover valid **location** values, use the [Get googlocations](#04a70a53-c58a-4084-8cde-3e1acd04dd57) endpoint.

response.name: Example

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Google Search Scraper (2)",
  "concurrency": 1,
  "export_unique_results": true,
  "to_complete": false,
  "no_line_breaks": true,
  "params": {
    "country": "United States",
    "language": "English",
    "max_pages": 4,
    "mobile_results": true,
    "results_per_page": 10,
    "location": "Alabama"
  }
}

response.body:
{
    "name": "Google Search Scraper (2)",
    "no_line_breaks": true,
    "params": {
        "country": "United States",
        "language": "English",
        "max_pages": 4,
        "mobile_results": true,
        "results_per_page": 10,
        "location": "Alabama"
    },
    "to_complete": false,
    "export_unique_results": true
}

request.description:
When you run a **Google Search Squid**, each returned “result” record follows this schema:

| **Field** | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **id** | integer | Unique identifier for this result |
| **object** | string | Always "result" |
| **squid** | string | Hash ID of the squid that produced this result |
| **run** | string | Hash ID of the run during which this result was scraped |
| **page** | integer | Search‐results page number (1-based) |
| **title** | string | The page title as shown in Google’s results |
| **url** | string | Canonical URL of the result |
| **displayed_url** | string | The “pretty” URL shown beneath the title |
| **description** | string | Snippet or meta‐description extracted from the page |
| **snippet_segments** | array | (Optional) tokenized segments of the snippet |
| **emphasized_keywords** | string (JSON) | JSON-stringified map of which keywords Google emphasized |
| **answer** | string or null | If Google provided a direct answer snippet (e.g. featured Q&A), otherwise null |
| **question** | string or null | Original query question for featured Q&A results, otherwise null |
| **date** | string or null | Publication date or date metadata if detected |
| **is_organic** | boolean | true for non-paid (organic) results |
| **is_paid** | boolean | true for ad or paid listings |
| **is_question_answer** | boolean or null | true if this is a featured “People also ask” or Q&A snippet |
| **is_related_query** | boolean or null | true if this entry represents a related search suggestion |
| **total_results** | string | Google’s reported total count of matching results (“0”, “42”, “1,234”, etc.) |
